LXQS-Z3Q: Joseph Phipps III was born in 1699 in Upper Providence Township in colonial Pennsylvania. By 1742 he had settled in Uwchlan Township, Chester County, where he made his home for many years. He later moved south and died in 1772 in Botetourt, Virginia.
LVYD-397: William Lea was born in 1654 in Charles City County, Virginia, in the early years of British Colonial America. He rose to a position of leadership and, by 1699, served as Commander in Chief of the King and Queen County Militia. His life ended in 1703 in St. Stephens Parish when he fell from a horse, cracked his skull, and died.
LT14-C7G: Maria Anna Barbara Braun was born in 1653 in the Allgäu region of Bavaria, Germany. She lived through to 1714, dying in the Palatinate of Bavaria. Surviving records of her birth, marriage, and death vary widely, reflecting the uncertainty of accounts from that era.
LBX8-7N7: Louise de Saix was born in 1648 in Gironde, France. Her life took her far from her homeland, and she died in 1688 in Bantry, County Cork, Ireland.
LZYD-L1W: Abigail Bachelder was born in 1637 in Charlestown, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She spent her life in the same town, where she died in 1693.
LJ5V-JCB: James Cannon was born in 1645 in Yorkshire, England, and crossed the Atlantic to make a new life in colonial Maryland. By 1688 he held land at a place called Cannon's Chance in Dorchester County. He died there in 1712, leaving a last will and testament that was lost for a time before being recorded in August 1723.
LTCZ-LC5: Henry Bramlett I was born in 1710 in Overwharton Parish, Stafford County, Virginia. He lived his life in colonial Virginia and died in 1756 in Prince William County.
9CH7-P66: Lady Mary Tudor was born in 1673 in Westminster, England, and rose to become the 2nd Countess of Derwentwater, holding the title of Duchess of Derwentwater. In 1700 she separated from her husband, Edward Radclyffe, the 2nd Earl of Derwentwater. She died in 1726 in Paris, France.
LTVV-XCG: Adam Vogelmann was baptized on 7 April 1644 in Michelfeld, in Württemberg, Germany. He married there on 3 November 1674 and raised his family in the region. He died in 1724 and was buried on 22 June of that year in nearby Westheim.
MYBQ-GPN: Mary Tilton was born in 1658 in Essex County, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. She made her home on Martha's Vineyard, where she outlived a second husband, who died in Tisbury in 1727. She lived a long life, dying there in 1746.
M3TF-47Z: William Johnston was born in 1649 at Caskieben in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. He left his native land for the American colonies and settled in Virginia, where he died in 1719, leaving a will dated 28 September of that year.
KN6C-QZK: Isaac Howe was born in 1648 in Sudbury, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony, and worked the land as a husbandman. He spent his life in colonial Middlesex County and died in 1724 in nearby Marlborough.
GX3D-45G: Andrew Fletcher was born in 1603 in Perthshire, Scotland, and rose to prominence as a knight, taking the title Lord Inverpeffer in 1623. By 1643 he resided at Saltoun Hall in Haddingtonshire, and in 1645 he was appointed Commissioner of the Exchequer for Scotland. He died in 1650 at Pencaitland.
LHC7-RZR: Johann Abraham Hertel was born in 1620 in Rathsweiler, in the Rhineland-Palatinate region of Germany. He lived his entire life in that village, where he died in 1691.
L7GV-X32: Private Abraham Whinery was born in 1764 in Orange County, North Carolina. He married there in February 1786 and appears in census records living in Orange County through 1810, before moving on to Bedford County, Tennessee, by 1820. He died that same year far to the west, in Gravette, Benton County, Arkansas.
L69Q-HDL: Anne Houston was born in 1680 at Ballygowan, near Drumbeg in County Down, Ireland. Few firm records of her life survive, and questions remain about whether she ever emigrated to America and who her children truly were.
LHFD-81X: Georg Michael Vogelmann was born in 1728 in Westheim, in Württemberg, Germany. He emigrated across the Atlantic and settled in Orange County, North Carolina, where he left a will in 1798. He died there in 1797, and his family later spread westward, with descendants recorded in Avoyelles Parish, Louisiana, by 1850.
L4Q8-937: Thomas Wicker Sr. was born in 1717 in St. Peter's Parish, New Kent County, Virginia. He later moved south to Chatham, North Carolina, where he died in 1784. He is recognized as a Patriot ancestor of the Daughters of the American Revolution.
L6GZ-K4Q: Edward Garrett was born in 1708 in Stafford, Virginia, and gave military service to the colony. He lived in colonial Virginia and died in 1751 in Loudoun County.
LJJ4-X1Q: John James Gilliland was born in 1706 in Carnmoney, County Antrim, Ireland, where the family name was spelled simply Gilli. After the family emigrated to America the name was changed to Gilliland. He settled in Chester, Pennsylvania, where he died in 1790.
LZGX-DY4: Colonel John Cushing was born in 1627 in Hingham, Norfolk, England, and crossed the Atlantic to the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He made his home in Scituate, where in 1692 he served as the town's first representative. He died there in 1708.
9S1J-6WB: Mary Carr was born in 1677 in Wiltshire, England, and was baptized at Warminster, possibly the daughter of Thomas Carr. She emigrated to Virginia, where she lived out her later years. She died in 1759 at Houstons Corner in Caroline County.
LBQV-T8P: Captain Thomas Owsley II was born before 27 March 1698 in Stafford County, Virginia, the son of Thomas Owsley. He resided in Stafford County in the 1720s before settling in Fairfax County. He died there in 1750, leaving a last will and testament and an estate that was inventoried and appraised in 1751.
LVQ6-SGQ: George Goring, 1st Earl of Norwich, was born in 1585 in Chevening, Kent, England. He died in 1663 in Brentford, Middlesex.
KLW3-9VJ: Captain Benjamin Bridges was born in 1664 in Topsfield, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He lived his life in colonial Massachusetts and died in 1725 in Framingham.
LBNV-MD5: James Travers was born in 1645 in Newbury, in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He took up arms in King Philip's War, serving in 1675 at Brookfield and again in 1676 in the Onondaga country of New York. He died in 1717 in Brookfield, Worcester County.
GH1R-5JQ: Richard Baker was born in 1630 in Chirton, Wiltshire, England, and spent some twenty-five years living in Barbados before relocating to South Carolina around 1681. He settled at Archdale in Dorchester County and, from 1696 to 1697, served as a representative for Berkeley and Craven Counties. He died in 1698 near Charleston.
LHFZ-2KG: Susannah Hammond was born in 1697 in Maryland. She lived until 1752.
